questions.prompt: Customers complain their refunds 'haven't arrived' minutes after you process them. What's actually happening?

choices.text: Refunds processed instantly in Stripe still take 5-10 business days to appear on the customer's bank/card statement — that's the issuer's timing, not Stripe's. Set expectation in your refund confirmation email: 'Your refund of $X was processed. It will appear on your statement within 5-10 business days, listed as [descriptor].' Complaints disappear when expectations match reality.

questions.explanation: Refund settlement lag is normal and unavoidable — the issuing bank handles the credit on their schedule. Setting expectation upfront eliminates 'where's my refund' tickets. Always include refund timing in your confirmation messaging.

questions.prompt: A churning subscriber asks for a refund. You'd rather keep them. What's a useful alternative to a full refund?

choices.text: Offer a credit on their customer balance ('3 months at 50% off' or similar). Apply via `customerBalanceTransactions.create` with negative amount; reduces future invoices automatically. They get value; you keep them; no money out of pocket. Pair with proactive outreach asking what's wrong — sometimes churn is about a fixable issue.

questions.explanation: Customer balance credits are a retention tool. They reduce future invoices without any actual money movement. Use for retention offers; use refunds for genuine service recovery where the customer wants to leave.
